skill.name: performance-engineer
skill.description: Expert performance engineer specializing in modern observability, application optimization, and scalable system performance. Masters OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing, load testing, multi-tier caching, Core Web Vitals, and performance monitoring. Handles end-to-end optimization, real user monitoring, and scalability patterns. Use PROACTIVELY for performance optimization, observability, or scalability challenges.
skill.summary: Improves application performance with observability, profiling, load testing, caching, and scalability planning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported three weak cryptography hits and one system reconnaissance hit. Review found only performance engineering prose and a safety warning about production load testing, with no scripts, commands, network calls, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The static finding at line 3 is a substring match inside the frontmatter description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, or encryption guidance is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line is descriptive skill metadata about performance engineering. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or insecure crypto recommendations.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The static finding at line 17 is normal text about designing load tests and scalability strategies. No cryptographic implementation or recommendation appears there.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is ordinary performance-planning prose. I found no evidence of weak algorithms such as DES, MD5, or SHA-1 used in context.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The static finding at line 176 is an example prompt about designing a load testing strategy. It does not mention cryptography or security-sensitive algorithms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The context is a user-facing example for load testing. I found no cryptographic code, algorithm selection, or insecure encryption advice.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The static finding at line 36 is a safety instruction to avoid load testing production without approvals and safeguards. It discourages unsafe operational behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line is a protective warning, not an instruction to enumerate systems or probe targets. No evidence of reconnaissance behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Slow applications lose users, waste infrastructure, and hide reliability risks. This skill guides measurement, profiling, load testing, caching, and monitoring for durable performance gains.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses backend, frontend, database, and infrastructure performance bottlenecks.
Designs load tests, capacity plans, performance budgets, and regression gates.
Recommends observability plans using traces, metrics, logs, and real user monitoring.
Improves caching strategies across application, database, CDN, browser, and API layers.
Prioritizes optimizations by user impact, business value, effort, and operational risk.
Plans validation steps with baseline metrics, rollout controls, and monitoring guardrails.
limitations: Requires metrics, traces, profiles, or test results for accurate diagnosis.
Does not run production load tests without explicit approvals and safeguards.
Cannot guarantee gains without implementation, measurement, and validation in the target system.
May need environment-specific knowledge for cloud, database, or runtime tuning.

use_cases.title: Improve API Latency
use_cases.description: Find slow endpoints, trace service calls, tune database access, and define measurable latency goals.

use_cases.title: Protect Web Vitals
use_cases.description: Analyze page load behavior, reduce render blockers, and set budgets for user-facing performance metrics.

use_cases.title: Plan Capacity Growth
use_cases.description: Create load scenarios, model scaling limits, and define monitoring for a high-traffic launch.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Slow Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this service performance issue. Identify likely bottlenecks, missing measurements, and the first three checks I should run.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Load Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a load testing plan for this system. Include traffic model, success metrics, test stages, data needs, and production safety controls.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Observability Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this observability setup for performance diagnosis. Recommend traces, metrics, logs, dashboards, alerts, and sampling decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Scalability Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scalability roadmap for this architecture. Prioritize bottlenecks, caching layers, database changes, rollout steps, and validation metrics.

output_examples.input: Our checkout API p95 latency doubled after a release.
output: Baseline current p50, p95, p99, error rate, throughput, and dependency latency.
Compare traces before and after the release to isolate the changed span.
Check database query plans, cache hit rate, connection pool saturation, and downstream retries.
Roll back or canary a targeted fix, then add an alert for the affected journey.

output_examples.input: We need a launch readiness plan for expected traffic growth.
output: Define realistic traffic scenarios from current usage and launch forecasts.
Run staged load tests with ramp, soak, spike, and recovery phases.
Set pass criteria for latency, errors, saturation, queue depth, and autoscaling behavior.
Document capacity limits, rollback triggers, and monitoring coverage before launch.

output_examples.input: Our React app has poor Core Web Vitals.
output: Measure LCP, INP, CLS, JavaScript cost, image weight, and third-party impact.
Prioritize critical resource loading, image optimization, code splitting, and hydration cost.
Add performance budgets and regression checks in the release workflow.

best_practices: Start with baseline metrics and user impact before changing code or infrastructure.
Validate each optimization with repeatable tests and production monitoring.
Add performance budgets, alerts, and dashboards to prevent regressions.
anti_patterns: Optimizing without measurements or a clear performance goal.
Running load tests against production without approvals, limits, and rollback plans.
Adding cache layers without ownership, invalidation rules, and observability.

faq.question: What systems can this skill help optimize?
faq.answer: It covers frontend, backend, databases, cloud infrastructure, mobile, edge, and distributed systems.

faq.question: Does it require existing observability data?
faq.answer: It works best with metrics, traces, logs, profiles, or load test results.

faq.question: Can it design load tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define scenarios, traffic models, success criteria, stages, and safety controls.

faq.question: Can it help with Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: Yes. It can analyze LCP, INP, CLS, resource loading, JavaScript cost, and regression budgets.

faq.question: Will it make production changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It recommends plans, checks, and guardrails. Changes require user approval and implementation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains guidance only and no executable scripts, network calls, or unsafe instructions.
